title: -Submitting Application for Permanent Local Housing Allocation Program

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager or designee to apply for and accept
funds from the State of California's Permanent Local Housing Allocation Program, in an
amount not to exceed $849,945.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The City of Berkeley is currently eligible to apply for one year of funds through the
State’s Permanent Local Housing Allocation (PLHA) program, not to exceed $849,945,
Fund #302. The funding would be used for Housing and Community Services (HCS)
programs in support of lower income and/or unhoused residents.
sections.current_situation:
The City will apply for up to $849,995 in PLHA funds through California Department of
Housing and Community Development’s (HCD) Notice of Funding Availability released
in October 2024. The funding ($849,995) represents Year 5 of the City’s five-year PLHA
plan amendment submitted in 2024 and the agreement signed in early 2025. This
application includes a Year 5 spending plan for a continued application of funds to
support existing HCS programs, including the Housing Trust Fund (HTF) program and
homeless services.
PLHA funds can be used to support a variety of planning or implementation activities
related to housing and community development carried out by HCS staff within the
Department of Health, Housing, and Community Services. HCS staff anticipate using
the funds for the following eligible activity categories: 1) The predevelopment,
development, acquisition, rehabilitation, and preservation of multifamily, residential live-
work, or rental housing that is affordable to extremely low-, very low-, low-, and
moderate-income households, including necessary operating subsidies; and/ or 2)
Assisting persons who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, including providing
rapid rehousing, rental assistance, navigation centers, emergency shelters, and the new
construction, rehabilitation, and preservation of permanent and transitional housing,
along with grant administration (within the established limit of funds).
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099

sections.background:
PLHA is intended to provide a permanent, ongoing source of funding to local
governments for eligible housing-related activities and programs that assist in
increasing the supply of affordable homes in California and addressing the unmet
housing needs of their local communities. Funds are generated from a $75 recording
fee on certain real estate transaction documents, so the amount allocated through the
program varies year to year. PLHA is noncompetitive, with funds allocated to cities
based on the Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) distribution formula.
HCD allocated a total of $7,475,827 to the City during the five-year period. To access
the funds, the City is required to have a five-year PLHA Plan, amend it when needed,
and submit streamlined applications for each year of funding. The City used funds from
Years 1 and 2 to support the operating of the navigation center (STAIR) and for Golden
Bear Homes – a Homekey project. For Years 3 and 4, the City allocated a total of
$965K to operating subsidies for homeless households such as shelter operations and
navigation services and allocated an estimated $2.2M to the HTF program and will be
used to support rehabilitation of occupied projects in the HTF portfolio. Up to 5% of
each year’s PLHA allocation is available to the City for admin to support program
implementation.
For Year 5, consistent with the amended and signed PLHA plan, the City will allocate
45% of the funds (approximately $382K) to operating subsidies for homeless
households and 50% (approximately $425K) to the HTF program. The remaining 5%
(approximately $42K) will be used to reimburse staffing costs.
The City is required to use HCD’s resolution template for the application and the plan
amendment for this Council item.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no environmental sustainability and climate impacts directly associated with
the recommendations in this report.
sections.rationale:
If approved, an application for PLHA funds will make up to $849,995 available for
Berkeley to support City programs that serve lower income or unhoused populations
sections.alternatives:
The City could decline to participate in the PLHA Program. Since that would not be
consistent with the City’s Strategic Plan to increase affordable housing opportunities,
staff are recommending participation as described above.
CONTACT PERSON
Asavari Devadiga, Community Development Project Coordinator, HHCS, (510)
981-5426

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
A RESOLUTION OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF BERKELEY
AUTHORIZING APPLICATION FOR AND ADOPTING THE PERMANENT LOCAL
HOUSING ALLOCATION PLAN FOR THE PERMANENT LOCAL HOUSING
ALLOCATION PROGRAM
WHEREAS, the California Department of Housing and Development (“Department”) is
authorized to provide up to $296 million under the SB2 Permanent Local Housing
Allocation Program Formula Component from the Building Homes and Jobs Trust Fund
for assistance to Cities and Counties (as described in Health and Safety Code section
50470 et seq. (Chapter 364, Statutes of 2017 (SB 2)); and
WHEREAS, the State of California (the “State”) Department issued a Notice of Funding
Availability (“NOFA”) dated 10/15/2024 under the Permanent Local Housing Allocation
(PLHA) Program; and
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley (the “Applicant”), is an eligible Local government who
has applied for program funds to administer one or more eligible activities, or a Local or
Regional Housing Trust Fund to whom an eligible Local government delegated its PLHA
formula allocation; and
WHEREAS, the Department may approve funding allocations for PLHA program, subject
to the terms and conditions of the Guidelines, NOFA, Program requirements, the
Standard Agreement, and other contracts between the Department and PLHA grant
recipients.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley THAT:
SECTION 1. If Applicant receives a grant of PLHA funds from the Department pursuant
to the above referenced PLHA NOFA, it represents and certifies that it will use all such
funds in a manner consistent and in compliance with all applicable state and federal
statutes, rules, regulations, and laws, including without limitation all rules and laws
regarding the PLHA Program, as well as any and all contracts Applicant may have with
the Department;
SECTION 2. Applicant is hereby authorized and directed to receive a PLHA grant, in an
amount not to exceed the five-y
